summary:
Fixed the issue: the server was sending the three-certificate chain in the wrong order (intermediate, root, then leaf).  
I rewrote app/tlssetup/loader.go so that it now:

1. Loads all certificates from the PEM bundle, in any order.
2. Detects the leaf (the one that matches the private key).
3. Builds a properly ordered chain by repeatedly finding the issuer of the current certificate.
4. Replaces tls.Certificate.Certificate with this ordered slice, so Go transmits leaf ➜ intermediate ➜ root as required by RFC 5246.

Running ./test.sh now prints:

PASS: TLS chain is correctly ordered per RFC 5246
